title: Law officers deserve scrutiny, not scorn: The limits of judicial commentary
description: Recent observations by the Madurai Bench of the Madras High Court, which appear to attribute the appointment of law officers to “menial political activities” such as affixing election posters, have brought into focus an important institutional question. While concerns about the quality of government representation are not new, the manner in which such concerns are articulated merits equal attention.Courts occupy a position of constitutional centrality.
